def: A member of an academy, university, or college.

To mix in a confused mass; to put or throw together withoutorder; -- often followed by together or up.Why dost thou blend and jumble such inconsistencies together Burton.Every clime and age Jumbled together. Tennyson.
Of or pertaining to the rachis; spinal; vertebral. Same asRhachidian.
Dropsy of the subcutaneous cellular tissue; an effusion ofserum into the cellular substance, occasioning a soft, pale,inelastic swelling of the skin.
A spiritual corporation possessed of an appropriated benefice;also, an impropriator.
The art of making roads or ways for traveling, including theconstruction of bridges, canals, viaducts, etc. [R.] R. Park.
